hypothesis: If an LLM-driven materials design agent is augmented with external tools (memories, periodic table, knowledge base) and closed-loop feedback from a diffusion crystal-structure generator and a learned formation-energy evaluator, then it will more frequently generate valid, unique, and novel inorganic materials that meet specified formation-energy targets than tool-free LLM variants and diffusion-only baselines, because tool-guided reasoning and iterative property feedback focus the search on thermodynamically favorable regions of chemical space.
